Hi Steph,

It was so nice speaking with you today, and I am thrilled you enjoyed your first session enough to lock in for the year.

I am happy to confirm that we have increased your session time to 50 minutes at a cost of $210 + GST per session. This will include 20 minutes for Junior Kindy and 30 minutes for Kindy. Please note that sessions will continue through school holidays.

I am also pleased to confirm you are all set with Coach Hannah, continuing fortnightly from your original start date. We are so glad to have you with us!
As a friendly reminder, we maintain a four-session notice policy for any cancellations.

I will follow up with you after your first session to gather your feedback, but please feel free to reach out to us at any time prior if you have questions.


Kind Regards,

I love that she offered sessions with our nursery room too as we struggle to find incursions for them at times.

Thank you!

Thank you for your call this morning

 

Some of the feedback we have received is

 

. Educators feel that with the under 2 -3 age groups it feels a little overwhelming they often loose interest and play with other equipment and roam around the playground they start following each other, the instruction that are set out for the younger ones don’t seem to be age appropriate and on the day the lady seemed a little frustrated and flustered. 

. The under 2 age group children really like the basketball skills and loved the running with the ladders, but the lady seems o be overwhelmed when the children loose interest would be good if she can give simpler instructions

. The older children loved it some children that hadn’t participated in other weeks got in and gave it a go last week

 

I’m sorry if this wasn’t too descriptive but this is the feedback we have received so far

 

I hope this helps

Have a good day

Thank you

patterns.prevention:
• Assign a primary coach to each centre with a named backup to eliminate coverage gaps and ensure consistency week-to-week.

• Audit all centres starting in the past 8 weeks for early warning signs (missed sessions, rescheduled bookings, low completion rates) and contact managers directly to troubleshoot.

• Create a coach scheduling template that prevents single-coach reliance and flags centres where more than three different coaches have been assigned in one term.

• Require centres to confirm coach continuity plans in writing before session one, with escalation to state manager if changes occur mid-term.

patterns.prevention:
• **Assess** coach engagement levels at each centre within 5 days using a simple observation checklist (energy, punctuality, interaction quality) and flag any performers below standard immediately.

• **Confirm** with centres their coach continuity plans for the next 3 months—if a coach is temporary, communicate the replacement plan upfront and introduce backups before transitions occur.

• **Review** program content difficulty and equipment adequacy at at-risk centres by requesting parent feedback and adjusting sessions to match the age group's skill level.

• **Schedule** bi-weekly check-ins with centres showing early warning signs (low bookings, parent complaints, attendance gaps) to address concerns before cancellation notices arrive.

patterns.prevention:
• Contact centres that switched providers within the last 3 months to understand specific gaps in service (timing, program content, pricing) and document findings.

• Schedule check-ins with active centres at the 6-week mark to address any scheduling or program concerns before they escalate to cancellation.

• Review pricing and package flexibility with leadership to identify if we're positioned competitively against in-house alternatives.

• Conduct exit interviews with cancelling centres to capture detailed feedback on what the replacement provider offers that we didn't, then share insights across all state managers.

centres.name: Papilio Early Learning Reedy Creek
centres.closedDesc:
Affinity  Group -direction to cut all incursions immediately at center as already over budget. waived 4 session cancellation to assist with confirmation in writing we will be preferred provider as soon as incursions are back email confirmation

Date Started: 28/11/2025
Number of Sessions Completed: 22
Sessions Missed: 0
Rescheduled: 0

Coaches: Mataus ,Patsy

Summary of their time with us: 
The first week of sessions was described as going well, with the coach, Mateu  noted for being polite, professional, and on time, and there were no concerns raised at this early stage. As the program moved into December 2025, feedback remained stable and positive, with no additional bookings requested at that point but still no issues identified, reinforcing that the program had been well received from the outset.

In January 2026, minor operational adjustments were made to better suit the centre’s schedule, including trialling session times between 1:00 pm and 2:30 pm, which were later confirmed as workable. This period reflected a practical alignment phase where the service was being fine-tuned to fit the centre’s routine while maintaining strong satisfaction. By February 2026, direct feedback from the centre continued to be highly positive, with Mateus again described as “great” and “amazing,” consistently on time, and with no complaints raised, indicating growing confidence in the program’s reliability.

In March 2026, further internal coordination took place, including a confirmed adjustment to a 2:00 pm start time, showing continued responsiveness to centre needs. Around the same time, broader feedback highlighted very strong satisfaction, with both coaches Mateus Gomes and Patricia (Patsy) Del Valle being praised for their energy, engagement, and ability to connect with children. Staff noted that the children were actively enjoying the sessions, and the coaches were described as having positive, bubbly personalities and being highly attentive and professional.

By April 2026, the program had fully stabilised and was operating at a consistently high level, with explicit feedback confirming that Mateus was engaging well with children and delivering sessions that were both enjoyable and well-received. Staff reiterated that children loved the activities and that the coach was a strong positive influence in the program, with no concerns raised at this stage.

In May 2026, feedback continued to reflect satisfaction with the program, with the centre confirming they were happy with the sessions and the coach, although Aletia was noted as being away at the time. Shortly after, the centre made the decision to cancel the program due to budget constraints rather than any issues with delivery or satisfaction. The final communication acknowledged that the service had been well liked and that both the coach and sessions had been appreciated, with an indication that they may reconnect in future when budget allows.

Overall, the timeline reflects a consistently positive service experience from start to finish, characterised by strong coach performance, high child engagement, smooth operational adjustments, and stable satisfaction, with the only reason for discontinuation being external financial constraints rather than program quality or delivery issues.

Next point of contact: purely budget happy to have us back asap when they have budget
signals: AI reasoning: Head office directive to cut all incursions immediately. Clean history: 22 sessions, zero missed, positive feedback on coaches.

preCloseStats.tone_reason: Centre is very happy with coaches Mateus and Patsy; children enjoying sessions; no concerns raised.

Following the Christmas period, sessions resumed in January 2026, with final schedule details confirmed on 20 January. On 28 January 2026, more detailed feedback was received from the centre regarding Coach Mateus, with educators noting that while he was well-liked and engaged positively with children, sessions appeared repetitive, lacked visible progression between age groups, and required stronger structure and preparation. However, Patsy's unexpected unavailability resulted in further disruption, and on 11 February 2026 the centre raised concerns after Mateus attended alone when a transition had been expected. During this period, Mini Monstars also worked closely with the centre regarding Affinity invoicing processes and payment procedures, helping navigate several administrative challenges between February and April. On 30 April 2026, after the centre advised they were experiencing budget pressures, Mini Monstars proposed a significantly reduced session structure and discounted pricing model in an attempt to retain services while reducing costs. She reiterated that the decision was not related to program quality, expressed how much the centre loved Coach Aisa and the sessions, and advised they would like Mini Monstars to return as soon as budgets allowed in the new financial year. Overall, Papilio Early Learning Robina's journey included an initially challenging coaching period that was successfully resolved through decisive action and a coach change, resulting in a highly positive experience under Coach Aisa.
